steps.title: Calculate the retail price
steps.content:
The retail price is 240% of the wholesale price.
$$\text{Retail price} = 2.40 \times 8.00 = 19.20$$
The retail price is $19.20.

steps.title: Apply the discount using the complement percentage
steps.content:
The key to speed: 70% off means the customer pays the remaining 30% of the retail price. Don't calculate the discount amount separately—multiply the retail price by 0.30 directly.
$$\text{Discounted price} = 0.30 \times 19.20 = 5.76$$
The discounted price is $5.76.
